senses.definition: a playing area built outdoors for children, particularly inside parks or schools

examples.x: The children spent the afternoon playing at the playground.
The school built a new playground with a climbing wall.
Parents watched their kids run around the playground.
The playground includes swings, slides, and a sandbox.
Safety mats were installed under the equipment in the playground.

senses.definition: an indoor play area filled with a large quantity of small, colorful plastic balls, often found in amusement parks, playgrounds, and children's entertainment centers, where kids can jump, play, and interact within the soft and bouncy environment

examples.x: The children spent hours playing in the ball pit at the indoor play center.
I found my son buried under a mountain of colorful balls in the ball pit.
The ball pit was full of laughing kids, each trying to find their way through the balls.
After climbing the slide, she dove straight into the ball pit.
We took the kids to a restaurant with a ball pit, and they had so much fun.

senses.definition: an object or structure that is designed to be filled with air or gas to make it rigid and buoyant, often used for recreational purposes like inflatable toys, bounce houses, or inflatable watercraft
a long metal frame consisted of horizontal and vertical rods that children can climb on

examples.x: The kids spent hours climbing on the jungle gym at the park.
She loves to challenge herself by climbing to the top of the jungle gym.
The jungle gym is always busy with children waiting for their turn to climb.
We could see the jungle gym from the picnic table where we were sitting.
He fell off the jungle gym but got back up and kept playing.

senses.definition: a structure consisting of a set of stairs leading up to a slope that children can slide down from

examples.x: The playground had a bright red slide that all the children lined up to use.
She climbed to the top of the slide and zoomed down with a big smile on her face.
The park installed a new, safer slide with soft padding at the bottom.
During the summer, the kids spent hours playing on the slide in the backyard.
The toddler hesitated at the top of the slide but eventually slid down with a little encouragement.

senses.definition: a seat hung by overhead chains or ropes on which people can sit on and move back and forth, often seen in playgrounds
a small, usually outdoor, structure or house-like play area designed for children, often made of wood or plastic, providing a space for imaginative and creative play activities

examples.x: The children spent the afternoon in their playhouse, pretending to run a store.
We bought a wooden playhouse for the backyard so the kids could have their own space to play.
After lunch, the kids ran to the playhouse and started acting out a scene from their favorite show.
The playhouse was painted in bright colors, making it look like a little fairy tale cottage.
When it rains, the kids still play in the playhouse, as it keeps them dry and entertained.

senses.definition: a playground equipment consisting of a series of horizontal bars set at different heights, where children can hang, swing, and climb across, promoting upper body strength and coordination during play

examples.x: The children were having fun swinging across the monkey bars at the playground.
She climbed up to the top of the jungle gym and raced her friend across the monkey bars.
After school, the kids always run straight to the monkey bars to see who can go the fastest.
The monkey bars are too high for me, but I love watching others do tricks on them.
He was tired after playing on the monkey bars for so long, but he did not want to stop.

senses.definition: a piece of playground equipment made of a long flat piece of wood or iron balanced in the middle, on each side of which children sit to take a turn going up and down
a large machine in amusement parks that revolves around a fixed center and has seats in the shape of animals or cars that people can ride on
a shallow container or a hole in the ground that is filled with sand and is large enough for children to play in
a playground equipment designed for young children, featuring a seat or platform mounted on metal springs that allow the rider to bounce and rock back and forth in a fun and safe manner

examples.x: The children enjoyed riding the spring rider at the park, laughing as it bounced up and down.
She pushed off the ground with her feet, making the spring rider move faster.
The spring rider was shaped like a horse, and he pretended to gallop across the playground.
He loved how the spring rider felt like a mini roller coaster when it moved quickly.
The younger children were excited to try the spring rider for the first time and couldn't wait for their turn.

senses.definition: a play equipment with various climbing elements, promoting physical activity and imaginative play

examples.x: The children spent hours playing on the climbing structure at the park.
The new climbing structure has slides and tunnels, making it even more exciting.
After playing on the climbing structure, they ran over to the swings to take a break.
She climbed all the way to the top of the climbing structure without any help.

senses.definition: a type of swing made from a recycled tire, typically suspended by ropes or chains from a tree branch or playground structure, offering a fun and classic swinging experience for children and adults alike
a playground equipment made of sturdy ropes or webbing, woven together to create a mesh-like structure, providing a challenging and exciting climbing experience for children

an outdoor recreational activity and adventure sport where participants ride along a cable or wire, often suspended between two elevated points, using a harness and pulley system, experiencing a thrilling and fast-paced aerial ride

examples.x: The kids couldn’t wait to try the zip line at the adventure park.
The zip line goes over the entire playground, offering a thrilling view from above.
I’ve never been on a zip line before, but it looks thrilling.
The zip line took us high above the forest, giving us an amazing view.
After climbing up to the platform, he grabbed the harness and took off on the zip line.

senses.definition: a children's toy that is normally shaped like a horse and attached to rockers with a seat on it, designed to rock a child back and forth

examples.x: The child happily rocked back and forth on the rocking horse in the living room.
She gently pushed the rocking horse, making it sway side to side.
His favorite toy was the wooden rocking horse he received for his birthday.
The rocking horse was the first toy her parents bought for her after she learned to walk.
The little girl held onto the reins of the rocking horse as she pretended to ride through the countryside.

senses.definition: a piece of exercise equipment that consists of a stretchy cloth attached to a frame on which people can jump for fun or exercise

examples.x: The children spent the afternoon bouncing on the trampoline in the backyard.
Gymnasts use the trampoline to practice their aerial maneuvers and flips.
Safety nets around the trampoline help prevent accidents while jumping.
The trampoline park was filled with people of all ages enjoying the various bouncing activities.
After installing a trampoline in their garden, the family noticed an increase in their outdoor playtime.

senses.definition: an outdoor water play activity that involves a long, narrow sheet of plastic or tarp, usually with water running down its surface, allowing participants to slide along it for a fun and wet experience
